nodes.name: Validate response

options.systemPromptTemplate:
You are a highly intelligent and accurate sentiment analyzer. Analyze the sentiment of the provided text. Categorize it into one of the following: {categories}. Use the provided formatting instructions. Only output the JSON.

> Evaluate the following customer support response. Give a short JSON answer with a field “quality”: “pass” or “fail”. Only return “pass” if the response:

1. Acknowledges both the broken keyboard and the late delivery  
2. Uses a polite and empathetic tone  
3. Offers a clear resolution or next step (like refund, replacement, or contact support)

nodes.notes: This sentimentAnalysis node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Generate response

messageValues.message: =You’re an AI assistant replying to a customer who is upset about a faulty product and late delivery. The customer uses sarcasm and is vague. Write a short, polite response, offering help.

nodes.notes: This chainLlm node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
### Customer complaint - example

I really *love* waiting two weeks just to get a keyboard that doesn’t even work. Great job. Any chance I could actually use the thing I paid for sometime this month?

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content: Defines the LLM node by index which should be used.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content: Dynamically connects the LLM by the index provided in the previous node.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note3

parameters.content: Check if LangChain Code Node ran into error. _Currently only supports error output from main Node_

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note4

parameters.content: Generates a polite answer based on the customers complaint.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note5

parameters.content: Analyses the generated answer by certain criteria

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note6

parameters.content: Increases the index to choose the next available LLM on the next run

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

parameters.content:
### Customer complaint - example

I really *love* waiting two weeks just to get a keyboard that doesn’t even work. Great job. Any chance I could actually use the thing I paid for sometime this month?
Defines the LLM node by index which should be used.
Dynamically connects the LLM by the index provided in the previous node.
Check if LangChain Code Node ran into error. _Currently only supports error output from main Node_
Generates a polite answer based on the customers complaint.
Analyses the generated answer by certain criteria
Increases the index to choose the next available LLM on the next run

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Manual Trigger".
Open the "When chat message received" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Switch Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
